description: Bengali romanisation lookup table. Keys are transliteration strings. Use 'matchOrder' for greedy (longest-first) matching when parsing a roman input string.

o.notes: Primary vowel. As o in British English 'hOt'. Default inherent vowel after every consonant unless otherwise indicated. Can be written ó to distinguish from /o/.
As o in English 'Obey'. Can be written ô to distinguish from /ɔ/.

ʼ.notes: Silent অ o after a consonant that is NOT the last letter in a word.
Silent ও ou, occurring only (but not necessarily) immediately before w.

examples.meaning: to be, to become, to exist

eo.notes: অ o pronounced as a in English 'rAt'. Occurs only after a silent y in the first syllable of a word (prefixes and compounding ignored).

ö.notes: Non-traditional grapheme for transliterating non-subcontinental words. As the vowel in French 'dEUx' (/ø/) or 'OEUvre' (/œ/), approximately as u in English 'hUrt'. Formed by jzo-phola used as diacritical mark with অ o.

a.notes: As a in English 'fAther'. Called স্বর-আ svoro-a. Written as আ-কার a-kar (া) after a consonant.

ea.notes: স্বর-আ svoro-a pronounced as a in English 'rAt'. Occurs (i) after a silent y in the first syllable of a word, or (ii) in any syllable after gjnj.

ä.notes: Non-traditional grapheme for transliterating non-subcontinental words. As a in English 'rAt'. Formed by jzo-phola + া a-kar used as diacritical mark with অ o.

i.notes: As i in English 'bIt'. Called হ্রস্ব-ই hrosvo-i (short I). Written as হ্রস্ব-ই-কার hrosvo-i-kar (ি) before the consonant it follows.

ii.notes: In careful pronunciation as i in English 'machIne'; colloquially as /i/. Called দীর্ঘ-ঈ diirgho-ii (long II). Written as দীর্ঘ-ঈ-কার diirgho-ii-kar (ী) after a consonant.

u.notes: As u in English 'fUll'. Called হ্রস্ব-উ hrosvo-u (short U). Written as হ্রস্ব-উ-কার hrosvo-u-kar (ু) fused below the consonant it follows.

ü.notes: Non-traditional grapheme for transliterating non-subcontinental words. As the vowel in French 'rUE' (/y/). Formed by jzo-phola used as diacritical mark with হ্রস্ব-উ hrosvo-u.

uu.notes: In careful pronunciation as u in English 'rUde'; colloquially as /u/. Called দীর্ঘ-ঊ diirgho-uu (long UU). Written as দীর্ঘ-ঊ-কার diirgho-uu-kar (ূ) fused below the consonant it follows.

rwi.notes: As ri in English 'gRIn'. Treated as a semi-vowel; never occurs before a vowel; no inherent অ o implied after a final ঋ. Written as ঋ-কার rwi-kar (ৃ) fused below the consonant it follows.
Exceptional: pronounced as IPA /rri/ (as re-ri in English 'caRE-RIdden') immediately after হ h when হ h occurs after a vowel (the হ h will be silent).

rwii.notes: Non-existent in modern Bengali. As ri in English 'marINe'. Treated as a semi-vowel; no inherent অ o implied after a final ৠ. Written as ৠ-কার rwii-kar (ৄ) fused below the consonant it follows.

examples.meaning: archaic verb stem: to scatter, to disperse
archaic verb stem: to praise, to gulp

lwi.notes: Non-existent in modern Bengali. As li in English 'gLIb'. Treated as a semi-vowel; no inherent অ o implied after a final ঌ. Written as ঌ-কার lwi-kar (ৢ) fused below the consonant it follows.

examples.meaning: archaic verb stem: to envision, to arrange

lwii.notes: Non-existent in modern Bengali and absent from any Sanskrit word. As lee in English 'gLEE'. Treated as a semi-vowel; no inherent অ o implied after a final ৡ. Written as ৡ-কার lwii-kar (ৣ) fused below the consonant it follows.

e.notes: As e in English 'rEd'. Called এ e. Written as এ-কার e-kar (ে) before the consonant it follows.

ee.notes: এ e pronounced as a in English 'rAt'.

ë.notes: Non-traditional grapheme for transliterating non-subcontinental words. Most commonly as the vowel in French 'trEIze' (/ɛ/), approximately as ea in English 'bEAr'. Formed by jzo-phola used as diacritical mark with এ e.

ê.notes: Non-traditional grapheme for transliterating non-subcontinental words. As a in English 'rAt'. Formed by jzo-phola + া a-kar used as diacritical mark with এ e.

aoi.notes: Approximately as oi in English 'cOIn'. Called ঐ aoi. Note: আ a + অ o + ই i = ঐ aoi (sondhi rule). Written as ঐ-কার aoi-kar (ৈ) before the consonant it follows.

ou.notes: As o in Italian 'cellO'. Called ও ou. Note: অ o + উ u = ও ou (sondhi rule). Written as ও-কার ou-kar (ো) enclosing the consonant it follows.

aou.notes: As ow in English 'bOWler' or oa in English 'rOAd'. Called ঔ aou. Note: আ a + অ o + উ u = ঔ aou (sondhi rule). Written as ঔ-কার aou-kar (ৌ) enclosing the consonant it follows.

ngw.notes: As ng in English 'siNG'. Called অনুস্বর onusvor / অনুস্বার onusvar. Never occurs before a vowel or diphthong; invariably occurs after a vowel or diphthong. Not a consonant in the Romanisation (no inherent অ o implied).

hw.notes: Called বিসর্গ visorgo. Sounded only at the end of a word; always follows (never precedes) a vowel or diphthong. Not a consonant in the Romanisation (no inherent অ o implied).
Silent, immediately before and doubling the pronunciation of a consonant letter.

nw.notes: Nasalisation of vowel or diphthong, as n in French 'viN' (/vɛ̃/). Called চন্দ্রবিন্দু condrovindu. Written in Romanisation next after the vowel or diphthong it nasalises. Not a consonant (no inherent অ o implied).

k.notes: As k in English 'Kid'. Called ক ko.

q.notes: As k in English 'Kid' but representing an original uvular ق /q/ sound in Arabic loanwords.

kh.notes: Approximately as kh in English 'sinKHole' and ckh in English 'duCKHouse'. Called খ kho.

g.notes: As g in English 'Gas'. Called গ go.

gh.notes: Approximately as gh in English 'doGHouse'. Called ঘ gho.

ng.notes: As ng in English 'fiNGer'. Called উঙ ungo or উম umo.

c.notes: Approximately as c in Italian 'Cello' or ch in English 'beaCH'. Called চ co.

ch.notes: Approximately as 'tch h' in English 'maTCH Head' or 'chh' in English 'beaCHHead'. Called ছ cho.

j.notes: Approximately as gi in Italian 'adaGIo' or j in English 'aJar'. Called বর্গীয় জ vorgiiyo jo (generic J).

z.notes: IPA /z/ (as z in English 'Zebra') or /ʥ/ (as gi in Italian 'adaGIo'), representing an original sound close to z in English 'Zebra' or th in English 'THe' in non-subcontinental loanwords.

gj.notes: IPA /g/. Occurs only and invariably before ঞ nj; this juxtaposition occurs only in words derived from the archaic verbal stem জ্ঞা gjnjea ('to know', 'to be aware of').

examples.meaning: knowable, an object to be known

jh.notes: Approximately as dgeh in English 'briDGEHead'. Called ঝ jho.

nj.notes: As the first consonant element in nh in Portuguese 'seNHor' or ñ in Castilian Spanish 'seÑor'. Called ঞীয় njiiyo or ইঙ ingo.
Silent, immediately after and (other than at the start of a word) doubling the pronunciation of জ gj.

examples.meaning: something that should be known, worth learning

td.notes: As t in English 'Trick' but retroflexed. Called মূর্ধন্য ট muurdhonyo tdo (coronal TD). Romanisation follows that for ড dt.

tdh.notes: Approximately as th in English 'hoTHead' but retroflexed. Called মূর্ধন্য ঠ muurdhonyo tdho (coronal TDH). Romanisation follows that for ড dt.

dt.notes: As d in English 'carDinal' but retroflexed. Called মূর্ধন্য ড muurdhonyo dto (coronal DT).

examples.meaning: to be submerged, to be drowned

rr.notes: Called ড শূন্য র dto shuunyo ro; a variant of ড muurdhonyo dto. IPA /r/ (as r in English 'aRid' or rr in English 'huRRy'). In earlier/careful pronunciation retroflexed as /ɽ/.

dth.notes: Approximately as dh in English 'maDHouse' but retroflexed. Called মূর্ধন্য ঢ muurdhonyo dtho (coronal DTH). Romanisation follows that for ড dt.

rrh.notes: Called ঢ শূন্য র dtho shuunyo ro; a variant of ঢ muurdhonyo dtho. IPA /r/ (as r in English 'aRid' or rrh in English 'cataRRH'). In earlier pronunciation retroflexed /ɽ/; originally retroflexed and aspirated /ɽʱ/.

nh.notes: Retroflexed n, when next to retroflex consonants ট td, ঠ tdh, ড dt, and (in theory) ঢ dth. Original pronunciation. Called মূর্ধন্য ণ muurdhonyo nho (coronal NH). In careful pronunciation always retroflexed.
IPA /n/. As alveolar n in English 'Net'. The usual pronunciation in modern Bengali.

t.notes: As the dentalised t in French 'méTier' or Italian 'andanTe'. Called দন্ত্য ত dontyo to (dental T).

t-.notes: Called খণ্ড ত khonhdto to (separated T); not an independent letter. Represents ত t followed by ্ hosonto (ত t without its inherent অ o). Does not follow a consonant or precede a vowel; exists at the end of a word or before a consonant/conjunct with which ত t has no combining form.

th.notes: Approximately as th in English 'hoTHead' but dentalised; similar to th in English 'THin'. Called দন্ত্য থ dontyo tho (dental TH).

d.notes: As the dentalised d in French 'maDame' or Italian 'Dolce'. Called দন্ত্য দ dontyo do (dental D).

dh.notes: Approximately as dh in English 'maDHouse' but dentalised; similar to th in English 'THen'. Called দন্ত্য ধ dontyo dho (dental DH).

n.notes: As the dentalised n in Italian 'andaNte', when next to dental consonants ত t, থ th, দ d, and ধ dh. Original pronunciation. Called দন্ত্য ন dontyo no (dental N). In careful pronunciation always dentalised.
IPA /n/. As the alveolar n in English 'Net'. The usual pronunciation in modern Bengali. In practice, the difference between dentalised /ṋ/ and alveolar /n/ is so small it may be ignored.

p.notes: As p in English 'raP'. Called প po.

ph.notes: Approximately as ph in English 'uPHold'. Called ফ pho. Used in Bengali words derived from Greek or Greek-based words (not f).

f.notes: IPA /f/ (as f in English 'Fat') or /pʰ/, representing an original f sound in non-subcontinental loanwords. Preferred pronunciation is /f/ in personal names of non-subcontinental origin; otherwise /pʰ/ is usual.

b.notes: As b in English 'Bid'. Called ব bo or বর্গীয় ব vorgiiyo bo / vorgyo bo (generic B).
Silent, doubling the pronunciation of the preceding m. Occurs exceptionally in সম্বন্ধ sombondho ('relationship') and words derived from it.

bh.notes: Approximately as bh in English 'aBHor'. Called ভ bho.

m.notes: As m in English 'Market'. Called ম mo.

Silent, immediately after and (other than at the start of a word) doubling the pronunciation of ত t, দ d, ল l, শ sh, ষ x or স s. Note: ম m is NOT silent at the start of the suffix মান্/মতী man-/motii ('possessing'), even when added to words ending in those consonants.

jz.notes: As gi in Italian 'adaGIo' or j in English 'aJar', but more palatalised. Called অন্তঃস্থ য ontohwstho jzo.
Jzo-phola (য-ফলা) pronounced as /ʥ/. Occurs only after হ h when হ h occurs after a vowel (the হ h will be silent).

y.notes: Called অন্তঃস্থ অ ontohwstho o; a variant of য ontohwstho jzo. IPA /j/ (as y in English 'maYor') between vowels, or IPA /ɪ/ (as y in English 'awaY') otherwise.

Jzo-phola (য-ফলা) silent, doubling the pronunciation of an immediately preceding consonant. Default jzo-phola behaviour after a consonant (other than r after a vowel). Exceptions: after h (following a vowel) pronounced as jz; after r (following a consonant) pronounced as /ɪ/.
Jzo-phola (য-ফলা) pronounced as /ɪ/ (as e in English 'nEar'). Occurs only after র r when র r occurs after a consonant.

w.notes: Called অন্তঃস্থ অ ontohwstho o. IPA /w/ as in English 'aWake'. Occurs only when preceded by (i) উ u, (ii) ঊ uu, or (iii) ও ou (which may or may not be silent). Written as ৱ wo in Assamese.

r.notes: As r in English 'aRid'. Called অন্তঃস্থ র ontohwstho ro (inner R). Written as ৰ in Assamese. When preceded by a vowel and followed by a consonant, written above that consonant as রেফ reph. When preceded by a consonant and followed by a vowel or jzo-phola, written below as র-ফলা ro-phola (～).

l.notes: As l in English 'aLarm'. Called অন্তঃস্থ ল ontohwstho lo (inner L). When occurring immediately after another consonant, may be written as ল-ফলা lo-phola below that consonant.

v.notes: Called অন্তঃস্থ ব ontohwstho vo. Represents Sanskrit व v (IPA /ʋ/, midway between v and w) or original v/w sounds in loanwords. In modern Bengali, merged with বর্গীয় ব vorgiiyo bo (b). Written as ব-ফলা vo-phola fused to the preceding consonant when following অন্তঃস্থ consonants, ড় rr, ঢ় rrh, or prefix/prefixed-word ending in a consonant.
Exceptional: when immediately after অন্তঃস্থ হ ontohwstho ho (silent h), this ব v is pronounced as if it were উব ub. Occurs only in words deriving from the archaic verbal stem হ্বে hve ('to summon').

Silent (and doubles the pronunciation of the preceding consonant) when occurring immediately after any consonant other than: অন্তঃস্থ consonants (র r, ল l, ব v, হ h), ড় rr, ঢ় rrh, or a prefix/prefixed-word ending in a consonant. Written as ব-ফলা vo-phola fused to the preceding consonant.

sh.notes: As sh in English 'wiSH' but palatalised. Original pronunciation, invariably palatalised when next to palatal consonants চ c, ছ ch, জ j, ঝ jh, and ঞ nj. Called তালব্য শ talovyo sho (palatal SH).

examples.meaning: free from holes, without pores, compact

sh.notes: IPA /s/. As s in English 'Slow'. Occurs when next before ঋ rwi, র r, or ল l.
IPA /ʃ/. As sh in English 'waSH'. The usual pronunciation in modern Bengali.

x.notes: As sh in English 'harSHer' but retroflexed. Original pronunciation, when next to retroflex consonants ট td, ঠ tdh, ড dt, and ঢ dth. Called মূর্ধন্য ষ muurdhonyo xo (coronal X).
IPA /kʰ/. Approximately as kh in English 'sinKHole'. Occurs only and invariably immediately after ক k in the conjunct ক্ষ kx.
IPA /ʃ/. As sh in English 'caSH'. The usual pronunciation in modern Bengali.

s.notes: IPA /s/. As s in English 'Star'. Original pronunciation. Occurs (i) before dental consonants ত t, থ th, দ d, ধ dh, ন n; (ii) before ঋ rwi, মৃ mrwi, র r, or ল l; (iii) when representing original /s/ in English loanwords; (iv) at the start of a word before ক k, খ kh, প p, or ফ ph. Called দন্ত্য স dontyo so (dental S).
IPA /ʃ/. As sh in English 'caSH'. The usual pronunciation in modern Bengali.

h.notes: As h in English 'beHind'. Called অন্তঃস্থ হ ontohwstho ho.

Silent when: (i) immediately before ঋ rwi (ঋ then pronounced /rri/); (ii) between a vowel and jzo-phola (jzo-phola then pronounced as jz); (iii) before any other consonant (that consonant's pronunciation is doubled). Exceptional: ব v after silent হ h is pronounced as উব uv (not doubled) in words from archaic stem হ্বে hve.
